description: This figure presents initial electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) characterization results of Fe–30Mn–10Al–1.5C–xBe (x = 0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, denoted as 0Be, 0.5Be, 1.0Be, 1.5Be respectively) low-density steels after hot rolling at 1050 °C. It includes 4 columns of subgraphs for each sample with varied Be content: the first column (a1/b1/c1/d1) are image quality (IQ) maps marking grain boundaries of different misorientation angles; the second column (a2/b2/c2/d2) are inverse pole figure (IPF) maps showing grain orientation; the third column (a3/b3/c3/d3) are kernel average misorientation (KAM) maps characterizing local strain and stress concentration; the fourth column (a4/b4/c4/d4) are phase distribution maps where green represents austenite and red represents ferrite. The scale bar is 100 μm for 0Be and 0.5Be samples, and 60 μm for 1.0Be and 1.5Be samples. Obvious trends are observed: ferrite content increases gradually with rising Be content, KAM maps show apparent stress concentration at precipitation sites of carbides and Be-Fe phases, while stress distribution is relatively uniform in high-Be (1.0Be, 1.5Be) samples. This figure supports the conclusion that Be addition regulates phase composition, precipitation behavior and micro-stress distribution of the low-density steels.
